Symptoms: Increased thirst, frequent urination, fatigue, blurred vision, slow healing wounds, numbness in hands or feet
Description: A chronic metabolic condition where the body cannot properly use insulin, leading to high blood sugar levels. It is the most common form of diabetes affecting millions worldwide.
Treatment: Lifestyle changes including diet and exercise, blood sugar monitoring, insulin therapy if needed
Cause: Insulin resistance, obesity, physical inactivity, unhealthy diet, genetic predisposition, age over 45
Prevention: Maintain healthy weight, exercise 30 minutes daily, eat low-sugar balanced diet, avoid processed foods, regular blood sugar screening
Medication: Metformin, Glipizide, Insulin, Januvia, Jardiance
Diet: Low glycemic foods, whole grains, leafy greens, avoid sugary drinks and white bread

Symptoms: Headache, shortness of breath, nosebleeds, dizziness, chest pain, vision problems (often no symptoms)
Description: High blood pressure condition where blood force against artery walls is too high. Known as the silent killer because it often shows no symptoms until serious damage is done.
Treatment: Lifestyle changes, low sodium diet, regular exercise, stress management, medication
Cause: High sodium diet, obesity, stress, lack of exercise, genetics, smoking, excessive alcohol
Prevention: Reduce salt intake below 2300mg/day, exercise regularly, quit smoking, limit alcohol, manage stress
Medication: Amlodipine, Lisinopril, Losartan, Hydrochlorothiazide, Metoprolol
Diet: DASH diet, fruits, vegetables, low-fat dairy, reduce sodium and saturated fats

Symptoms: Severe throbbing headache on one side, nausea, vomiting, sensitivity to light and sound, visual aura, dizziness
Description: A neurological disorder characterized by recurrent intense headaches often accompanied by sensory disturbances. Can last 4 to 72 hours and significantly impacts daily life.
Treatment: Rest in dark quiet room, pain medications, triptans, preventive medications for frequent migraines
Cause: Hormonal changes, stress, certain foods like cheese and wine, bright lights, lack of sleep, weather changes
Prevention: Identify and avoid personal triggers, maintain regular sleep schedule, stay hydrated, manage stress
Medication: Sumatriptan, Rizatriptan, Amitriptyline, Topiramate, Ibuprofen
Diet: Avoid aged cheese, red wine, chocolate, caffeine, MSG; stay well hydrated

Symptoms: Wheezing, shortness of breath, chest tightness, persistent cough especially at night or early morning
Description: A chronic inflammatory disease of the airways causing breathing difficulties. Airways become narrow and swollen, producing extra mucus making breathing difficult.
Treatment: Inhaled bronchodilators, corticosteroids, avoiding triggers, breathing exercises
Cause: Airborne allergens, respiratory infections, exercise, cold air, air pollution, smoke, dust mites
Prevention: Avoid triggers, use air purifiers, maintain clean environment, get vaccinated, monitor peak flow
Medication: Albuterol inhaler, Fluticasone, Montelukast, Salmeterol, Budesonide
Diet: Anti-inflammatory foods, vitamin D rich foods, avoid sulfites in dried fruits and wine

Symptoms: Chest pain, cough with green or yellow phlegm, fever with chills, shortness of breath, fatigue, loss of appetite
Description: Infection causing inflammation in one or both lungs. Air sacs may fill with fluid or pus. Can range from mild walking pneumonia to life-threatening illness.
Treatment: Antibiotics for bacterial pneumonia, antivirals for viral type, rest, fluids, hospitalization for severe cases
Cause: Bacteria especially Streptococcus pneumoniae, viruses including influenza and COVID-19, fungi
Prevention: Vaccination including pneumococcal and flu shots, good hand hygiene, not smoking, healthy diet
Medication: Amoxicillin, Azithromycin, Levofloxacin, Oseltamivir for viral type
Diet: High calorie nutrient rich foods, warm soups, hydration, vitamin C rich fruits

Symptoms: Sudden high fever up to 104F, severe headache, pain behind eyes, joint and muscle pain, skin rash, mild bleeding from nose or gums
Description: A mosquito-borne viral infection transmitted by Aedes aegypti mosquitoes. Causes flu-like illness and can develop into severe dengue which is life-threatening.
Treatment: Supportive care with rest and fluids, paracetamol for fever, avoid aspirin and NSAIDs, hospitalization for severe cases
Cause: Dengue virus transmitted through bite of infected Aedes aegypti or Aedes albopictus mosquito
Prevention: Use mosquito repellent, wear full sleeve clothing, eliminate stagnant water, use mosquito nets
Medication: Paracetamol, IV fluids in hospital, no specific antiviral exists
Diet: Plenty of fluids, papaya leaf juice may help platelet count, nutritious easily digestible foods

Symptoms: Persistent cough for more than 3 weeks, coughing up blood, chest pain, unintentional weight loss, night sweats, fever, fatigue
Description: Serious infectious bacterial disease primarily affecting the lungs. Spread through the air when infected person coughs or sneezes. Can be latent or active.
Treatment: 6 month antibiotic course with multiple drugs, directly observed therapy, isolation during contagious period
Cause: Mycobacterium tuberculosis bacteria spread through respiratory droplets in air
Prevention: BCG vaccination, early detection and treatment, good ventilation, covering mouth when coughing
Medication: Isoniazid, Rifampin, Pyrazinamide, Ethambutol — all four together for 2 months then two for 4 months
Diet: High protein diet, eggs, milk, meat, lentils to support weight gain and recovery

Symptoms: Cyclic fever with chills, headache, nausea, vomiting, muscle pain, sweating after fever breaks, fatigue, anemia
Description: Life-threatening parasitic disease spread by female Anopheles mosquitoes. Parasites multiply in the liver then infect red blood cells causing their destruction.
Treatment: Antimalarial drugs based on parasite type and severity, supportive care, hospitalization for severe cases
Cause: Plasmodium parasite especially falciparum and vivax species transmitted by Anopheles mosquito bite
Prevention: Insecticide-treated bed nets, indoor spraying, antimalarial prophylaxis when traveling, mosquito control
Medication: Artemether-Lumefantrine, Chloroquine, Quinine, Doxycycline for prevention
Diet: Iron-rich foods to combat anemia, plenty of fluids, easily digestible foods during fever

Symptoms: Fatigue and weakness, pale or yellowish skin, irregular heartbeat, shortness of breath, dizziness, chest pain, cold hands and feet
Description: A condition where you lack enough healthy red blood cells to carry adequate oxygen to your body tissues. Many types exist including iron deficiency anemia which is most common.
Treatment: Treat underlying cause, iron supplements, vitamin B12 or folate supplements, blood transfusion in severe cases
Cause: Iron deficiency, vitamin B12 deficiency, folate deficiency, chronic disease, blood loss, bone marrow problems
Prevention: Balanced diet rich in iron and vitamins, treat underlying conditions, regular health checkups
Medication: Ferrous sulfate, Vitamin B12 injections, Folic acid supplements, Erythropoietin for chronic disease anemia
Diet: Iron-rich foods like spinach, lentils, red meat, fortified cereals, vitamin C to enhance iron absorption

Symptoms: Nausea, vomiting, watery diarrhea, abdominal cramps and pain, low grade fever, muscle aches, headache
Description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines commonly called stomach flu. Usually caused by viral or bacterial infection and spreads easily through contaminated food or contact.
Treatment: Oral rehydration therapy, rest, bland diet, antiemetics for vomiting, antibiotics only for bacterial causes
Cause: Norovirus and rotavirus most common, bacteria like Salmonella and E. coli, contaminated food or water
Prevention: Frequent handwashing especially before eating, safe food handling, clean drinking water, rotavirus vaccination
Medication: ORS oral rehydration salts, Ondansetron for nausea, Loperamide for diarrhea, antibiotics if bacterial
Diet: BRAT diet: bananas rice applesauce toast, clear broths, avoid dairy and fatty foods until recovered

Symptoms: Fatigue and sluggishness, increased cold sensitivity, constipation, pale dry skin, brittle nails, hair loss, weight gain, depression, slow heart rate
Description: Condition where thyroid gland does not produce enough thyroid hormone. This slows down many body functions. More common in women and people over 60.
Treatment: Synthetic thyroid hormone levothyroxine taken daily, regular TSH blood tests to monitor levels
Cause: Hashimoto thyroiditis autoimmune disease, thyroid surgery, radiation therapy, iodine deficiency, certain medications
Prevention: Adequate iodine intake through diet, regular thyroid screening especially women over 35, avoid excessive soy
Medication: Levothyroxine, Liothyronine, Armour Thyroid natural desiccated thyroid
Diet: Iodine-rich foods like seaweed and seafood, avoid excessive soy and raw cruciferous vegetables

Symptoms: Fatigue, swelling in legs ankles and feet, decreased urine output, shortness of breath, nausea, confusion, persistent itching
Description: Progressive loss of kidney function over months or years. Kidneys filter waste and excess fluids from blood. When damaged waste builds up in the body.
Treatment: Control blood pressure and diabetes, low-protein diet, dialysis when kidneys fail, kidney transplant
Cause: Diabetes most common cause, hypertension, glomerulonephritis, polycystic kidney disease, repeated infections
Prevention: Control blood sugar and blood pressure, healthy diet, avoid nephrotoxic drugs like NSAIDs, stay hydrated
Medication: ACE inhibitors, ARBs for blood pressure, Erythropoietin for anemia, Phosphate binders
Diet: Low protein diet, limit potassium phosphorus and sodium, adequate calories to maintain weight

Symptoms: Persistent sadness and emptiness, loss of interest in activities, sleep disturbances, appetite changes, fatigue, difficulty concentrating, feelings of worthlessness, suicidal thoughts
Description: A serious mood disorder causing persistent feelings of sadness and loss of interest. Affects how you feel think and behave and can lead to various emotional and physical problems.
Treatment: Antidepressant medications, psychotherapy especially CBT, lifestyle changes, exercise, support groups
Cause: Genetics, brain chemistry imbalance, trauma or abuse, chronic illness, stress, major life changes
Prevention: Regular exercise, strong social connections, stress management, adequate sleep, avoiding alcohol
Medication: SSRIs like Sertraline Fluoxetine, SNRIs like Venlafaxine, Bupropion, talk therapy alongside medication
Diet: Omega-3 fatty acids from fish, folate from leafy greens, avoid alcohol, regular meals

Symptoms: Fever, dry cough, fatigue, loss of taste or smell, shortness of breath, body aches, sore throat, headache, diarrhea
Description: Infectious respiratory disease caused by SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus. Ranges from mild illness managed at home to severe disease requiring hospitalization and intensive care.
Treatment: Supportive care at home for mild cases, antiviral drugs for high-risk patients, oxygen and ventilation for severe cases
Cause: SARS-CoV-2 virus spread through respiratory droplets aerosols and contact with contaminated surfaces
Prevention: COVID-19 vaccination, wearing masks in crowded places, frequent handwashing, ventilating indoor spaces
Medication: Paxlovid for high-risk patients, Remdesivir in hospital, Dexamethasone for severe inflammation
Diet: High protein foods for recovery, vitamin C and zinc to support immunity, plenty of fluids

Symptoms: Joint pain worse with activity, stiffness especially in morning, swelling around joints, grating or crackling sensation, reduced range of motion, bone spurs
Description: Degenerative joint disease where cartilage breaks down causing bones to rub together. Most common form of arthritis affecting hips knees hands and spine.
Treatment: Pain management with NSAIDs, physical therapy, weight loss, joint injections, joint replacement surgery
Cause: Aging, joint injury or overuse, obesity, genetics, gender as women more affected, bone deformities
Prevention: Maintain healthy weight, regular low-impact exercise like swimming, protect joints from injury, good posture
Medication: Ibuprofen, Naproxen, Celecoxib, Topical diclofenac, Cortisone or hyaluronic acid injections
Diet: Anti-inflammatory diet with fish nuts berries, maintain healthy weight, calcium and vitamin D for bones

Symptoms: Burning sensation during urination, frequent urge to urinate, cloudy or strong-smelling urine, pelvic pain especially in women, blood in urine, lower back pain
Description: Bacterial infection affecting the urinary tract including bladder kidneys ureters and urethra. More common in women. Usually affects the bladder and urethra.
Treatment: Antibiotics for 3 to 7 days, increase fluid intake, phenazopyridine for pain relief, longer course for kidney infection
Cause: Bacteria especially E. coli entering urinary tract, sexual activity, menopause, urinary catheters, blockages
Prevention: Drink plenty of water, urinate after sex, wipe front to back, avoid irritating feminine products
Medication: Trimethoprim, Nitrofurantoin, Ciprofloxacin, Fosfomycin, Cephalexin
Diet: Plenty of water, cranberry juice may help prevent recurrence, avoid caffeine and alcohol during infection

Symptoms: Sudden pain starting around navel moving to lower right abdomen, pain worsening with movement, nausea, vomiting, fever, loss of appetite, abdominal bloating
Description: Inflammation of the appendix a small finger-shaped pouch attached to large intestine. Medical emergency requiring prompt surgical treatment to prevent rupture.
Treatment: Surgical removal of appendix (appendectomy) either laparoscopic or open surgery, antibiotics before and after
Cause: Blockage of appendix by hardened stool, foreign body, or infection leading to bacterial infection and inflammation
Prevention: No specific prevention known, prompt medical attention when symptoms appear to prevent rupture
Medication: Antibiotics before surgery, pain management, IV fluids
Diet: Clear liquids before surgery, soft easily digestible foods during recovery, high fiber diet after recovery

Symptoms: Itchy blister-like rash starting on chest face and back, fever, tiredness, loss of appetite, headache
Description: Highly contagious viral infection causing itchy blister rash. Caused by varicella-zoster virus. Usually mild in children but can be severe in adults and immunocompromised people.
Treatment: Calamine lotion for itching, antihistamines, antiviral acyclovir for severe cases or high-risk patients, keep nails trimmed
Cause: Varicella-zoster virus spread through direct contact with rash or through respiratory droplets
Prevention: Varicella vaccine is highly effective, avoid contact with infected individuals
Medication: Acyclovir, Valacyclovir for high-risk patients, Calamine lotion, Antihistamines for itching
Diet: Soft foods if mouth sores present, avoid acidic foods, plenty of fluids, popsicles for comfort

Symptoms: Lump or swelling in neck, trouble swallowing, voice changes or hoarseness, swollen lymph nodes, neck pain, difficulty breathing
Description: Cancer that develops in thyroid gland. Several types exist with papillary thyroid cancer being most common and having excellent prognosis when caught early.
Treatment: Surgery to remove thyroid, radioactive iodine therapy, thyroid hormone therapy, radiation, targeted therapy for advanced cases
Cause: Genetic mutations, radiation exposure especially in childhood, family history, certain inherited syndromes
Prevention: Avoid unnecessary radiation exposure to neck, genetic counseling for family history
Medication: Levothyroxine after surgery, Radioactive iodine I-131, Sorafenib or Lenvatinib for advanced cancer
Diet: Low-iodine diet before radioactive iodine treatment, adequate nutrition for recovery

Symptoms: Red patches of skin covered with thick silvery scales, dry cracked skin that may bleed, itching burning or soreness, thickened pitted nails, joint swelling and stiffness
Description: Chronic autoimmune skin condition causing rapid buildup of skin cells. Cells build up quickly on surface forming scales and red patches that can be itchy and painful.
Treatment: Topical treatments, light therapy phototherapy, systemic medications for severe cases, biologics
Cause: Immune system dysfunction triggering abnormal skin cell growth, genetics, stress, infections, certain medications
Prevention: Manage stress, avoid smoking and excess alcohol, keep skin moisturized, identify personal triggers
Medication: Topical corticosteroids, Methotrexate, Cyclosporine, Biologics like Adalimumab and Secukinumab
Diet: Anti-inflammatory diet, omega-3 fatty acids, vitamin D, avoid alcohol which can trigger flares
